key_concepts_learned: MLOps Maturity Levels: Manual → ML Pipeline Automation → CI/CD Pipeline Automation
Feature Stores: Feast (open source), Tecton (enterprise), Hopsworks (governance)
DVC: Data version control with .dvc metafiles, Git-like for large files
MLflow: Tracking (experiments), Model Registry (versions, aliases), Projects (packaging), Serving (deployment)
Pipeline Orchestration: Airflow (DAGs, mature), Prefect (Pythonic, lightweight), Dagster (asset-centric), Kubeflow (K8s-native)
CI/CD for ML: Code + Data + Hyperparameters versioning, CML for Git-based automation
Monitoring Layers: Service health → Model performance → Data quality → Drift detection
Data Drift vs Concept Drift: Input distribution change vs Input-Output relationship change
Deployment Strategies: Recreate (downtime), Blue-Green (zero downtime, 2x cost), Canary (gradual), A/B Testing (statistical)
Experiment Tracking Tools: MLflow (self-hosted), W&B (cloud, popular), Neptune (acquired by OpenAI), Comet

practical_applications: Implement MLflow para tracking e model registry
Setup DVC para data versioning em projetos ML
Considerar Feature Store para projetos com features compartilhados
Implementar monitoring com Evidently para drift detection
CI/CD pipeline com GitHub Actions + CML para ML projects
